intro.text:
You awaken in darkness. You remember the fall, the dried ground giving way beneath your heels, opening its maw and taking you in. You were documenting the new excavation site when the floor collapsed, or something like that.

It's honestly hard to remember.
Cragged stone digs against your back. The air smells of dust and something older — copper, perhaps, or blood long dried. Everything you came with is gone.

questions.text:
You kneel in the dust. A shard of glass catches the dim light. You pick it up and peer into its cracked surface.

What do you notice first?

choices.text: The weight of it in your palm, surprisingly heavy
How the light fractures and dances across its edges
Your own eye, staring back at you, unblinking
The darkness pooling in the deeper cracks

questions.text:
You tilt the glass. Your reflection shifts.

What troubles you about your own face?

choices.text: It looks gaunt, hollowed by something you cannot name
The eyes seem too quick, darting to things not there
There is a hardness in the jaw you do not remember
It smiles when you do not

questions.text:
Behind your reflection, in the deeper glass, something moves.

What do you believe it to be?

choices.text: A trick of the light, nothing more
The shadow of what you might become
Something that has always been watching
Your hands tighten. It does not matter what it is.

questions.text:
The cracks in the glass form a pattern.

What shape do they suggest to you?

choices.text: A web, radiating outward from a single point of impact
A river delta, branching and rebranching endlessly
Lightning frozen in glass, violence made still
The roots of a great tree, clutching at stone

questions.text:
You set down the glass. But before you do—

What do you whisper to your reflection?

choices.text: "I will endure."
"I will be swift."
"I will not falter."
"I will break what breaks me."

results.pre_stats: The glass falls from your fingers and shatters into a thousand smaller pieces. You rise. Somehow, you know yourself better now.
results.post_stats: Beside you lies a weapon. You do not question why it is there.
